title: aira caryophyllea var. capillaris
intro: Aira caryophyllea var. capillaris, also known as Wood Meadow-grass, is a type of grass belonging to the family Poaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce inflammation, and fight off infections. The health benefits of Wood Meadow-grass include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a valuable herb for treating various health conditions. Wood Meadow-grass can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for use as a natural remedy, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses.
